subject: Social Science (CBSE - Social and Political Life– II)

activities_projects.title: Compare Equality Movements
activities_projects.objective: Analyse different equality movements in India
steps: List at least 3 major equality movements discussed in the chapter
Compare their causes, methods, and outcomes
Create a table summarizing similarities and differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand patterns and strategies in social movements
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness, accuracy, and comparison clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Letter to a Social Reformer
steps: Choose a social reformer or activist from the chapter
Write a letter asking questions about their struggles and motivations
Include your reflections on their work
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect emotionally with historical figures

activities_projects.title: Equality Debate
steps: Divide class into two groups
One group argues that equality is fully achieved today, the other that it is not
Prepare arguments with examples from history and present
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop argumentation and reasoning skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Newspaper Article Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Link historical struggles with current events
steps: Find a recent news article about inequality or social justice
Write a short summary linking it with chapter concepts
Reflect on lessons learned from historical struggles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect theory with current society

activities_projects.title: Case Study on Child Rights
activities_projects.objective: Understand challenges faced by children in achieving equality
steps: Research one case of child labour or education inequality
Write a short case study including background, problems, and solutions
Present it in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain awareness of ongoing equality challenges
activities_projects.evaluation: Research depth and presentation clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster Campaign on Modern Equality
steps: Create a poster promoting equality in schools or communities
Include slogans and illustrations
Present and explain your poster to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students use creativity to promote social messages
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, message impact, and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interview a Local Activist
activities_projects.objective: Understand practical work in promoting equality
steps: Prepare 5–6 questions about their work and challenges
Conduct an interview
Write a report highlighting key insights
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students relate classroom learning to real-life activism
activities_projects.evaluation: Question relevance and report quality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Storyboard on Social Justice
steps: Choose one equality struggle from the chapter
Create a 4–6 panel storyboard showing key events
Include dialogues or captions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop storytelling and visual thinking skills

activities_projects.title: Mock Election for Equality Committee
steps: Form a class committee for promoting equality
Hold elections with nominations and voting
Discuss rules, responsibilities, and action plan
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students experience democratic processes and responsibilities
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and understanding of democratic principles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Critical Reflection Essay
steps: Write a 200–250 word essay reflecting on whether your school/community practices equality
Include suggestions for improvement
Use examples from chapter and personal experience
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking and writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of reflection, examples, and clarity (10 marks)
